reasoning: The stock just had a massive 1-day rally on the forecast date (March 14), pushing it up ~4% to $446.23, which forms a local peak relative to the last 2 weeks (hovering around 430). This automatically makes it harder to achieve a 'higher' price in the very short term due to mean reversion. Additionally, EG will go ex-dividend for a $2.00 quarterly payment on Monday, March 17 (the exact start of the forecast period), which introduces an instant mechanical headwind of ~$2 to the share price. Furthermore, the stock has been suffering from a slew of recent analyst downgrades (some with price targets in the 340-389 range) due to a $1.7 billion Q4 reserve charge that caused a large loss. Despite these headwinds, the company is generating strong operational cash flows and has authorized a massive 10 million share buyback, which will provide downside support and long-term tailwinds. Balancing the short-term negative mean reversion, the $2 ex-dividend reduction, and analyst downgrades against historical stock market drift and strong buybacks, I assign lower probabilities for the near-term dates and gradually revert towards a long-term base rate of 55%.
